steps.title: Combine appearance and revision
steps.content: Text 1 says researchers initially thought the fossil might be an avian dinosaur because its skull features looked birdlike. Text 2 agrees that the skulls looked avian at first, but says detailed studies found lizard traits.

steps.title: Keep both points together
steps.content: B is wrong because Text 2 makes clear why the initial thought arose. C reverses the mistake by saying the researchers initially assumed a lizard connection. D adds an unsupported size-and-shape comparison between skulls. The likely response would acknowledge that the birdlike interpretation made sense at first but was revised. The answer is A.
